title: The Theoretical Final Solution to the Eva Size Conundrum
display_title: The Theoretical Final Solution to the Eva Size Conundrum
wikitext:
[]: The (Theoretical) Final Solution to the Eva Size Conundrum!             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Fri Nov 08, 2013 5:12 pm I have, at long last, discovered a way to once and for all decide the "proper" height of the Evangelions from the original series. And it shall be done...with merchandise. First, we find out a pilot's height, in this case Shinji. His height is stated to be 155 cm. Next, we find a model of Unit 01's Entry Plug, like this: Using the knowledge that Shinji is 155 cm, we can work out the size of the Entry Plug. Finally, we need a figure of an Evangelion that comes with a removable Entry Plug. So, for example, the Soul of Chogokin SPEC: With the knowledge of the actual size of the Entry Plug, we could compare it to the Evangelion figure (which to my knowledge is the most accurate figure of original series Shougouki available) and finally find out exactly how big it really is! Unfortunately, I have none of the above items and my maths sucks, so I cannot provide the definitive answer myself. But now, at least, we know how we COULD find the answer.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Fri Nov 08, 2013 5:28 pm Herr Squigsquasher, I have never been a fan of Final Solutions.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Nov 08, 2013 5:41 pm gatotsu911 wrote: Herr Squigsquasher, I have never been a fan of Final Solutions.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Fri Nov 08, 2013 5:47 pm doooooohohohohohohohohohohohoh

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Nov 08, 2013 5:48 pm It varies in the show. No amount of merchandise will fix that. The merchandise also varies. All you're doing is picking a particular moment or scene. There is not going to be a final solution. I remember doing this with the Gendo rescuing Rei scene + Bandai kit back on the trivialbeing forums. It was pointless then and given the show is set in stone it's still pointless now. It's far more reasonable to just retroactively apply the official NME size of 80m back to the original. At least with that number you have a teeny-tiny bit of authenticity.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Nov 08, 2013 5:51 pm We should do this for every single basis for scale and see how widely the results vary.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Nov 08, 2013 6:17 pm I was gonna suggest the only way this would have any meaning is if you do a meticulous run of every single useful reference and then milk a few statistics out of that, but assumed there isn't much motivation here for that given the many other started but incomplete community projects. Because you either do that or try to argue why one reference is more legitimate than the other.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Nov 08, 2013 7:19 pm Much as I am loath to say this, hasn't it been officially confirmed that they vary in size? I prefer it that way.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Nov 08, 2013 8:29 pm Chuckman wrote: Much as I am loath to say this, hasn't it been officially confirmed that they vary in size?

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Nov 08, 2013 9:15 pm What does "officially" vary mean? Did a single particular source say that they vary, or did multiple official sources inconsistently report the height?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Nov 08, 2013 9:47 pm Monk Ed wrote: What does "officially" vary mean? Did a single particular source say that they vary, or did multiple official sources inconsistently report the height?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Nov 08, 2013 9:50 pm When Asuka jumped on that one ship the Eva had to be way more than 200 meters tall.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Nov 08, 2013 10:11 pm The 40m sometimes get trotted around because of the Super Robot Wars games. In a few games that's the height given. However it should be noted SRW specs can be full of shit and at one point put Evas at only 15m tall, which is obsurd. SRW should not be considered either official or even sane. IMO even 40m is too small.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sat Nov 09, 2013 2:08 pm I think by "officially" confirmed to vary sizes means that they've been seen at sizes that are so far apart form one another i.e. jumping onto aircraft carriers or being as tall as skyscrapers.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Sat Nov 09, 2013 3:37 pm It should be noted that a lot of giant robots fluctuate in size depending on what the rule of cool demands. Even the supposedly "real robot" Gundams exhibit this behavior , not to mention wonkier super robots like Daitarn 3. It's one of the bigger robots out there, apparently 120 meters tall canonically, and here we see it dangling a perfectly normal snake . So what the Evas are doing is just a bog-standard genre thing, and the only unusual part is that the creators drew attention to it by making their official bullshit numbers fluctuate as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Nov 09, 2013 4:50 pm You don't get to bitch about scale until you have a giant robot transform into a locomotive and then into a space shuttle that can easily carry dozens of other giant robots of equal size while an aircraft carrier turns into a fighter jet to pursue them at the behest of a peterbilt cabover that transforms into a robot whose foot is equal in size to the truck he just transformed from.
summary: []: The (Theoretical) Final Solution to the Eva Size Conundrum!             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Fri Nov 08, 2013 5:12 pm I have, at long last, discovered a way to once and for all decide the "proper" height of the Evangelions from the original series. And it shall be done...with merchandise. First, we find out a pilot's height, in this case Shinji. His height is stated to be 155 cm. Next, we find a model of Unit 01&#

Next, we find a model of Unit 01's Entry Plug, like this: Using the knowledge that Shinji is 155 cm, we can work out the size of the Entry Plug. Finally, we need a figure of an Evangelion that comes with a removable Entry Plug. So, for example, the Soul of Chogokin SPEC: With the knowledge of the actual size of the Entry Plug, we could compare it to the Evangelion figure (which to my knowledge is the most accurate figure of original series Shougouki available) and finally find out exactly how big it really is! Unfortunately, I have none of the above items and my maths sucks, so I cannot provide the definitive answer myself. But now, at least, we know how we COULD find the answer.

Article: The Theoretical Final Solution to the Eva Size Conundrum
 There is not going to be a final solution. I remember doing this with the Gendo rescuing Rei scene + Bandai kit back on the trivialbeing forums. It was pointless then and given the show is set in stone it's still pointless now. It's far more reasonable to just retroactively apply the official NME size of 80m back to the original. At least with that number you have a teeny-tiny bit of authenticity.
